name: Agility CMS API
what_it_does: Agility CMS REST and GraphQL APIs for headless and hybrid CMS platform. Enables AI agents to fetch pages and content items, manage sitemaps, retrieve syndication data, and integrate Agility CMS into web publishing workflows. Agility CMS is known for its page management and hybrid headless approach combining structured content with page routing.
use_cases: Fetching page content and sitemap data from web publishing agents
Retrieving structured content items from headless content delivery agents
Managing content syndication across channels from content operations agents
Integrating Agility CMS content into personalization and preview workflows
not_for: High-volume content repositories without page structure requirements
best_when: A web development team using Agility CMS wants AI agents to fetch and manage page content, structured items, and sitemaps for JAMstack and headless web projects.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: CMS may contain draft or confidential content. Management API credentials provide write access.

auth.notes: Agility CMS uses GUID + API key authentication. Separate read-only Fetch API key and Management API key for write operations. Keys are instance-scoped.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: 1 user, limited content, 1 environment
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro and Enterprise plans with more users, environments, and API calls
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing vs larger headless CMS platforms. Canadian company.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan tier; CDN-backed content delivery

known_agent_gotchas: No public MCP server — REST or GraphQL integration required
GUID parameter required alongside API key for all requests
Separate fetch API key (read) and management API key (write) — use correct key for operation
Page management model differs from pure headless CMS — understand sitemap/page hierarchy
Documentation less comprehensive than larger headless CMS platforms
agent_readiness.security_notes: API key + GUID auth. SOC2, GDPR. Separate read/write keys. Canadian company — CA data residency option.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key + GUID auth. SOC2, GDPR. Separate fetch/management keys.
